通う: to go to and from (a place)
久しぶり: a long time (since the last time)
点く: to be lit (of a lamp, burner, etc.)
移る: to move (to another place or state)
進学: advancing to the next stage of education
訓: native Japanese reading of a Chinese character
刺さる: to stick into (of something with a sharp point)
土産: local specialty or souvenir bought as a gift while travelling
伝わる: to spread (of a rumour, news, etc.)
宛名: name and address (on an envelope, etc.)
いってきます: I'm off (and will be back later)
おかず: small dish (usu. one of several accompanying rice)
拝む: to assume the posture of praying
送り仮名: kana written after a kanji to complete the full (usually kun) reading of the word
開通: opening (of a new road, railway, etc.)
書取: writing down from other written material
兼ねる: to serve two or more functions or roles simultaneously
関西: Kansai (region comprising Kyoto, Osaka, Kobe and surrounding prefectures)
関東: Kantō (region comprising Tokyo and surrounding prefectures)
冠: traditional cap worn by Shinto clergy and courtiers
斬る: to kill (a human) using a blade (sword, machete, knife, etc.)
御無沙汰: not writing or contacting for a while
サークル: group with a common interest (e.g. students)
刺身: sashimi (raw sliced fish, shellfish or crustaceans)
シーズン: season (period in which regulated games are played)
下町: low-lying part of a city (usu. containing shops, factories, etc.)
受験: taking an examination (esp. for entrance to a school or university)
書道: calligraphy (esp. Asian calligraphy based on Chinese characters)
スカーフ: scarf (esp. a lightweight summer scarf worn by women)
総理大臣: prime minister (as the head of a cabinet government)
中旬: middle ten days of a month
中世: Middle Ages (in Japan esp. the Kamakura and Muromachi periods)
長男: eldest son (may be the only son)
退ける: to put something out of the way
捕る: to catch (a wild animal, fish, insect, baseball, etc.)
日時: date and time (of a meeting, departure, etc.)
狙う: to aim at (with a weapon, etc.)
年度: fiscal year (usu. April 1 to March 31 in Japan)
挟む: to hold between (e.g. one's fingers, chopsticks)
はめる: to fit (e.g. a pane into a frame)
判子: seal (used in lieu of a signature)
日日: date (of a planned event, act, etc.)
日にち: date (of a planned event, act, etc.)
ふくめる: to include (in a group or scope)
閉会: closure (of a ceremony, event, meeting, etc.)
見舞う: to visit and comfort or console
目指す: to aim at (for, to do, to become)
盛る: to serve (in a bowl, on a plate, etc.)
夕立: (sudden, heavy) shower (on a summer afternoon or evening)
幼稚園: kindergarten (in Japan, non-compulsory education from age 3 until primary school)
呼び出す: to call (someone) to (the door, phone, etc.)
理科: science (natural sciences and related fields, incl. mathematics, engineering, medicine, etc.)
どうにか: to in some way or other
特産: being produced in a particular region
沿線: places alongside a railway line, bus route, major thoroughfare, etc.
赴く: to go in the direction of
かぶれる: to develop a rash or inflammation
自在: being able to do as one pleases
当て字: kanji used as a phonetic symbol, instead of for the meaning
甘える: to behave like a spoiled child
束ねる: to tie up in a bundle
赴任: moving to a different location to start a new job
へりくだる: to deprecate oneself and praise the listener
綻びる: to come apart at the seams
見合い: meeting with a view to marriage
見計らう: to choose at one's own discretion
寄り: pushing back one's opponent while locked in close quarters
初耳: something heard for the first time
日の丸: red or gold circle representing the Sun
